question_text: An electromagnetic wave has a wavelength of 0.5 m. If all electromagnetic waves travel at 3 × 10⁸ m/s in a vacuum, calculate the frequency of this wave.
explanation: Using the wave equation: speed = frequency × wavelength, or f = v ÷ λ. Substitute the values: f = (3 × 10⁸ m/s) ÷ (0.5 m) = 6 × 10⁸ Hz. This frequency places the wave in the microwave part of the spectrum.
